name: Larry Ellison
founding_note: Co-founded 1977 with Bob Miner and Ed Oates as Software Development Laboratories; proxy labels him Founder.
notes: 346M of his 1.158B shares pledged as loan collateral (proxy-disclosed).
No published lifetime-giving total; ~$200M USC Ellison Institute, ~$1B Ellison Medical Foundation (closed 2013); Giving Pledge signatory.

name: Michael Dell
founding_note: Founded 1984 as PC's Limited from his UT Austin dorm room; sole founder.
notes: 41.4% of ALL classes: 246.8M Class A (89.2% of A, super-voting unlisted) + 36.3M Class C (NYSE-listed).
Giving = Michael & Susan Dell Foundation reported; Dec-2025 $6.25B 'Trump Accounts' pledge is future commitment, excluded.

name: Reed Hastings
founding_note: Co-founded 1997 with Marc Randolph as DVD-by-mail.
notes: 4.23M beneficial shares / 425.6M outstanding (proxy marks <1%); incl. 2.07M exercisable options.
No lifetime total published; documented: $120M HBCUs (2020), $100M Hastings Fund (2016), $200M City Fund (2018).

name: Marc Benioff
founding_note: Co-founded 1999 with Parker Harris, Dave Moellenhoff, Frank Dominguez (none Forbes billionaires).
notes: Giving is combined Marc & Lynne Benioff, >$1B milestone announced Oct 2025, much to UCSF Benioff Children's Hospitals.

name: Phil Knight
founding_note: Co-founded Blue Ribbon Sports 1964 with Bill Bowerman; renamed Nike 1971.
notes: DERIVED aggregate: direct 27.5M Class A + 35.8M Class B PLUS 226.75M Class A held via Swoosh LLC (listed as separate owner in proxy; Swoosh figure as of 2024-07-11 Form 4).
Family controls ~97% of Class A voting power.
Giving ~$2.4B Chronicle direct-to-charity over ~10y; OHSU alone >=2.7B over longer window.

name: Stephen Schwarzman
notes: Blackstone files NO DEF 14A (founder-controlled, no proxy solicitation) - source is FY2025 10-K Item 12.

0.522 = share of Blackstone Holdings Partnership Units (231.9M, exchangeable 1:1), NOT common stock; economic interest ~19% per Bloomberg - strict-stake display must NOT multiply 0.522 by BX public market cap.
Documented gifts: MIT $350M, Schwarzman Scholars >$575M, Oxford ~$222M, NYPL $100M, Yale $150M.
